summary: R2 (retire extractMentionedPaths) is sound and ZFC-clean. Consumer analysis: extractMentionedPaths is called ONLY at analyzer.go:544 inside PlanFidelity (sole def+call in the tree); no other consumer. PlanFidelity itself is invoked only in tests — the production harness path (scenario_test.go:78 NewReport(...,0)) passes a literal 0 and never calls PlanFidelity. Non-gating confirmed: PlanFidelityScore is only stored on the Report struct and printed in FormatText/JSON; no threshold/comparison/pass-fail reads it. Scenario pass/fail derives from WrongActions + scenario.Assertions, not the score. Structured-vs-retire justification is accurate: zero key_file_paths/KeyFilePaths refs under internal/harness (they live in internal/validate + internal/approve); the analyzer receives only a raw plan path, so plumbing structured frontmatter for a non-gating score is disproportionate — retirement is the right call. Not vacuous: PlanFidelity retains extractMentionedCommands + the empty-expectations short-circuit (returns 1.0), so a commands-only score remains defined and meaningful; spec wisely says retire the path TERM, not PlanFidelity entirely (the function still has the commands signal). ADR-0036 fit: removing a hard-coded extension heuristic that scrapes prose is exactly the ZFC violation ADR-0036 forbids; clean. Hermetic test: TestAnalyzer_PlanFidelity_HighScore/LowScore exist (analyzer_test.go:524/563) and are reworkable to pin commands-only behavior under filtered -run TestAnalyzer_PlanFidelity, never the slow TestLLM_* scenarios; both High (go/go test mentioned + run) and Low (ls, unmentioned) still separate on the commands term, and Scope + AC R2 explicitly require updating these tests. Non-blocking note: the path/path-basename match block at analyzer.go:554-569 plus expectedPaths must be removed together with the function so PlanFidelity stays internally consistent (commands-only); spec's R2 text covers this as 'rework PlanFidelity so it no longer depends on prose-scraped paths'.
